content:
- Executed task 31 (mdBook install + docs scaffold + git hygiene) per task file NOW EXECUTE plan.
- Added `tools/install-mdbook.sh` pinned to mdBook v0.4.40 with per-arch release asset selection and SHA256 verification; installs into `.tools/mdbook/bin/mdbook`.
- Bootstrapped `docs/` with mdBook, replaced stub chapter with minimal IA placeholders, and documented build/serve in-book.
- Added Makefile targets: `docs-build`, `docs-serve`, `docs-hygiene`, plus `ensure-mdbook`; updated `.gitignore` to ignore `/docs/book/` based on observed artifacts.
- Captured evidence under `.ralph/evidence/task-31-mdbook/` (gitignored) including mdbook init/help, docs build/serve logs, hygiene checks, and full gate logs.
- Ran all required gates successfully: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint` (with CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1, CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0, RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 where relevant).
- Marked task file completed with `<passing>true</passing>`, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed + pushed.
- Added cross-app learnings to `AGENTS.md` about mdBook `docs/book/` artifacts and aarch64 musl release assets.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and checked for any <passes>false</passes>, <passing>false</passing>, and <passes>meta-task</passes>.
- Observed all bug tasks currently have <passes>true</passes>/<passing>true</passing>; several non-bug tasks remain <passes>false</passes> (not_started).
- Chose next task as the unblocked follow-up to mdBook setup: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/32-task-author-complete-architecture-docs-with-diagrams-and-no-code.md and wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- inspected docs setup for framework, location, toc, diagram support, and architecture pages
- confirmed mdBook is configured with minimal scaffold under docs/src and generated output under docs/book
- found no mermaid/plantuml integration or diagram blocks configured yet; constraints include pinned local mdBook binary and generated-output git hygiene

- Subagent task: mapped high-level system architecture from repo modules for docs planning.
- Read runtime orchestrator, HA decide/dispatch, DCS worker/store, pginfo worker, process worker, API worker/controller, and both binaries.
- Produced concise component map + recommended diagram set focused on services/workers, control/data flow, DCS interactions, and failover model. Next: hand this map to parent task plan writer.

- Read progress log (empty/no output), loaded `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and read task 32 file.
- Spawned parallel subagents to research docs setup (mdBook), docs gates, and high-level architecture components (pgtuskmaster runtime + workers, DCS keyspace, fail-safe/split-brain themes).
- Inspected existing `docs/src/*` and confirmed they are placeholder stubs and SUMMARY is flat.
- Wrote a detailed multi-phase execution plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/32-task-author-complete-architecture-docs-with-diagrams-and-no-code.md`, including proposed new IA, required diagrams, and a plan for mermaid support + a no-code guard.
- Marked the plan with `TO BE VERIFIED` per protocol; next step is to do the deep skeptical verification pass and adjust at least one plan element before switching to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Reviewed current mdBook docs IA: SUMMARY has 5 flat pages and all pages are placeholders (introduction, architecture, components, operations, glossary).
- Checked docs/book.toml and Makefile docs targets; no Mermaid preprocessor configured yet and docs targets only enforce mdbook binary existence.
- Produced skeptical plan updates: tighten IA to architecture-first with explicit redirects, make Mermaid install+wiring deterministic, and add docs-lint/no-code guard integration into lint gates with low false-positive policy.
- Next immediate step: apply these concrete plan edits into task-32 execution_plan and switch TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE once accepted.

- Ran architecture reconnaissance for task-32 by reading runtime/HA/DCS/API modules plus e2e and BDD tests.
- Confirmed concrete components and loops: node startup planner + six long-lived workers (`pginfo`, `dcs`, `process`, `ha`, `debug_api`, `api`) joined under `tokio::try_join!`.
- Extracted HA phases and semantics from `ha/decide.rs`: trust gate to FailSafe when not FullQuorum, switchover path, rewind/bootstrap/fencing recovery, and split-brain handling driven by conflicting leader record.
- Verified DCS keyspace includes not only member/leader/switchover but also config and init lock keys; trust rules depend on etcd health + cache self-presence/leader-presence.
- Verified API surface: current supported routes include `/switchover`, `/ha/switchover`, `/ha/state`, fallback routes, and debug routes; legacy `/ha/leader` routes intentionally 404 in BDD tests.
- Collected concrete scenario anchors from multi-node/partition e2e tests to guide doc diagrams (planned switchover, unassisted failover, no-quorum fail-safe/fencing, split-brain checks).
- Next: deliver concise corrections to draft doc plan + terminology/diagram suggestions, explicitly steering away from code-level narration.
